classifier_reason: Bitcoin is an open-source layer-1 protocol with no central authority or company; canonical CG/CMC pages and the official site confirm there is no stable company mapping.
Ethereum is an open-source layer-1 protocol with no central owner; the Ethereum Foundation is the main nonprofit supporting protocol development and ecosystem funding.
Tether is a fiat-backed stablecoin; the official Tether site and issuance primer identify Tether Operations Limited as the issuer/operator.
XRP is the native token of the XRP Ledger, and Ripple’s official XRP pages describe Ripple as the company using and contributing to XRPL and XRP.
BNB was created by Binance and is now the utility token of the BNB Chain ecosystem.
USDC is a regulated stablecoin issued by Circle.
Solana is a layer-1 network supported by both Solana Foundation and Solana Labs.
TRON is a layer-1 network whose official site centers TRON DAO as the governing community organization.
Dogecoin is a community-driven open-source meme currency with no stable operating company identified.
Hyperliquid is an L1 + onchain trading protocol; the official site redirects to Hyper Foundation, but the ownership structure is not fully conventional.
LEO is the utility token of the Bitfinex platform and is tied to the Bitfinex/iFinex ecosystem.
Cardano is a layer-1 network whose ecosystem is anchored by Cardano Foundation, IOHK, and EMURGO.
Bitcoin Cash is a decentralized forked layer-1 network with no stable company entity identified.
Chainlink is an oracle platform with a clear corporate developer in Chainlink Labs.
Monero is an open-source privacy network with no stable company entity identified.
USDe is issued by the Ethena protocol and operated by Ethena Labs.
Canton is a public L1 blockchain whose creator and principal commercial operator is Digital Asset.
Dai is governed by the Maker Protocol/Sky ecosystem rather than a stable operating company.
Zcash has identifiable supporting entities in Electric Coin Company and the Zcash Foundation.
Stellar has a clear nonprofit steward in the Stellar Development Foundation.
USD1 is a fiat-backed stablecoin brand owned by World Liberty Financial and issued by BitGo Trust.
Litecoin is an open-source decentralized payment network with no stable company mapping.
PYUSD is a stablecoin issued by Paxos Trust Company for the PayPal ecosystem.
Avalanche is a layer-1 network with a clear company steward in Ava Labs.
Hedera is governed through Hedera Hashgraph, LLC and the Hedera Council.
Sui is supported by both Sui Foundation and Mysten Labs.
Bittensor is still decentralized, but OpenTensor remains the identifiable official steward in the current transition.
Shiba Inu is a meme/community token with no stable operating company identified.
MemeCore is an official creative-studio/blockchain project with a named operating ecosystem.
Toncoin is supported by the TON Foundation, which publicly stewards the network.
